person.name: Thomas Eaton
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Marriage and children«/b»
«b»Married:«/b» 
Isabel Baker, daughter of Richard and Agnes Baker. She married second a Nasson, possibly Thomas Nasson a church warden at Rowington, between 1580 and 1582.

«b»Children of Thomas Eaton and Isabel Baker:«/b»
Order uncertain, presented in order found in will.

1.) John Eaton.

2.) Mary Eaton. Named in father's will.

3.) Alice Eaton. Named in father's will.

4.) Jane Eaton. Named in father's will.

5.) Thomas Eaton. Under age 16 in father's will.

6.) Anne Eaton. Named in father's will.

«b»Death and Will«/b»
«b»Died:«/b» 
Between 30 April 1571 and 6 July 1571.
Dates will made and proved.

«b»Will:«/b» 
An abstract of Thomas Eaton's will follows (Consistory Court of Worcester, 1571 No. 83 (the microfilm is partly illegible):

Thomas 'Yeton" of 'Rownton" (ie: Rowington) last day of April 1571. To be buried in aforesaid parish churchyard. To poor people of Rownton on day of testator's buriel, 10s. To godchildren, 6d a piece. To son John Yeton, one featherbed, one mattress with bolsters (illegible) belonging to said bed. To 3 eldest daughters Mary, Alice and Jane, pewter vessel. To daughter Mary 40 marks within one year and one cow. To wife's 3 sisters Margery, Anys and Marye (illegible). To son Thomas Yeton, all testor's freeland in parish of Hatton and in the lordship of Beawsall (ie: Beausale) bought by Wyllm. Atwode of Tarnworthe and wife Dorythe. If he died before age 16 without heirs of his body, then to remarry to the right heirs of me the said (illegible). To daughter Alice 6 pounds out of rent of aforesaid lands in Beawsall. To daughter Jane rent of the testator's tenement in Lawston end for 9 years 'which tenement is now unto Jhon Reve the younger (illegible) those years to remarry to Anne my daughter. To Anthony Byrd, testator's 'fryse cote and a canvas dowbelet and my best (illegible) and to his wife 4 yards of cloth to make her a coat. To testor's servant Jane Byrd 3s. 4d. To testator's servant George Home, (illegible) pair of hose and a hat. To testator's servant Jone, 6d. To testator's sister Mary Crane, 10s. To Thomas Crane, 6s 8d. Residue to wife Isabell Eaton to bring up children.

Executors: wife and Humphrey Crane of Warwycke, who is to receive for his pains 3s. 4d; overseers: Thomas Atwod and Jhon Benet (to receive 2s. a piece) Witnesses: Thomas Atwod, Thomas Ley and George Hom(e). Inventroy taken 24 May 1571: 92 pounds, 11s, 2d.

Since most people were illiterate in this time it is surprising that this will exists in Thomas's hand at all. Importantly, he mentions his purchase of land in the nearby parish of Hatton which he then bequeathed to his eldest son John Eaton.

zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton是John Eaton的兒子，於1530年之前出生在英國瓦維其可爾洛文頓。他於1558年與Isabel Baker在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1571年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾洛文頓。

nameMeanings.name: Eaton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Eaton or Eton, such as Eaton Socon (Bedfordshire), Eaton (Cheshire), or Eton (Buckinghamshire), named from either Old English ēa ‘river’ or ēg ‘island, low-lying land’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
History: Nathaneal Eaton, born in Coventry, England, c. 1609, came to MA in 1637 and was the first head of Harvard College, in 1638–39.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton was born before 1530, in Rowington, Warwickshire, England as the son of John Eaton. He married Isabel Baker in 1558, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1571, in Rowington, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton est né avant 1530, à Rowington, Warwickshire, Angleterre, fils de John Eaton. Il a épousé Isabel Baker en 1558, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1571, à Rowington, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton wurde vor 1530 in Rowington, Warwickshire, England, als Sohn von John Eaton geboren. Er hat 1558 in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom Isabel Baker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb 1571 in Rowington, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton è nato il prima del 1530 nel Rowington, Warwickshire, England come figlio di John Eaton. Lui sposato Isabel Baker nel 1558, nel Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1571, nel Rowington, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Eatonは1530年以前にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアロウィントンでJohn Eatonの息子として生まれた。1558年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでIsabel Bakerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1571年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアロウィントンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton(은)는 1530년 이전 영국 워윅실르 로윙턴에서 John Eaton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1558년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에서 Isabel Baker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1571년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 로윙턴에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton родился перед 1530 г. в местности роуингтон, варвиккшир, Англия. Его отца звали John Eaton. Он умер в 1571 г. в местности роуингтон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
